rulings.Case Title: VICTOR GUZMAN VS. KRIEG KELLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For Petition To Compel Arbitration, Appendix
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 18, 2009, line 13. DEFENDANTS KELLER, KRIEG, SLOAN, REILLEY & ROMAN LLP'S Petition To Compel Arbitration. Petition granted. This action is stayed pending arbitration. The agreement substantially complies with the factors set forth in Armendariz vs. Foundation Health Psychare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal. 4th 83. The cost-sharing provision is unlawful, but the agreement is not so permeated with unconscionable provisions that it cannot be saved. The cost-sharing provision is severed from the arbitration agreement. (See McManus vs. CIBC World Markets Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App. 4th 76.) The agreement provides for a neutal arbitrator and does not limit plaintiff's remedies. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC VS. ARNOLD SCHWARZENEGGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 18, 2009, line 15, PETITIONER BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate. Motion denied. The doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction not applicable. The Sacramento County and Alameda County cases involved different parties, and different allegations. Further, defendant did not raise the issue by filing a motion for stay or a demurrer/plea in abatement. Government Code sections 19851 and 19849 give the Executive broad authority to control the work week. No showing that the Governor's executive orders were ultra vires. The Governor acted reasonably in furloughing all employees to save funds and preserve parity. The DPA's decision not to exempt petitioner was also reasonable to preserve funds and maintain parity. =(302/CWW)
